title: Wi-Fi Troubleshooting: Can't Connect to Corporate Network
text:
Q: What should I do if I can't connect to the corporate Wi-Fi network?
A: If you're having trouble connecting to the corporate Wi-Fi network, follow these steps:

Step 1: Ensure Wi-Fi is enabled on your device.

Windows:
- Click on the Wi-Fi icon in the system tray (bottom right corner).
- Make sure 'Wi-Fi' is turned on.

macOS:
- Click on the Wi-Fi icon in the menu bar (top right corner).
- Ensure 'Turn Wi-Fi On' is selected.

Step 2: Check if you are within range of the corporate network. Move closer to the router if necessary.

Step 3: Forget and reconnect to the network.

Windows:
- Go to 'Settings' > 'Network & Internet' > 'Wi-Fi' > 'Manage known networks'.
- Select the corporate network and click 'Forget'.

macOS:
- Go to 'System Preferences' > 'Network' > 'Wi-Fi' > 'Advanced'.
- Select the corporate network and click the '-' button to remove it.

Step 4: Reconnect to the network by selecting it from the available networks and entering the password if prompted.

Step 5: If you still cannot connect, restart your device and try again.

Step 6: If the issue persists, check with your IT department to ensure there are no outages or restrictions affecting your access.
